name: finalize-req
description: Review and validate requirement JSON as a human-review candidate without locking.
auto_start_prompt: Begin executing the loaded TODO plan now. Task 1 is already in_progress — review and repair the req/*.json files with real tool actions, then let the review-candidate gate command run, then produce the handoff summary. This produces a human-review candidate only; do NOT lock or approve. Do not wait for further confirmation.

tasks.activeForm: Reviewing requirement quality before human review
tasks.detail: Direct-final is allowed: /finalize-req must be runnable WITHOUT a prior /draft-req. If any of the six req JSON candidates are missing, author them first, directly from the requirement source (req/$ATLAS_ACTIVE_IP_requirements.md, req/requirements.md, or the requirements stated in chat) under the same content rules as draft-req — do not demand a draft step, do not write locked_truth.md or approval_manifest.json. Then continue with the review below. Read req/requirements_index.json, req/obligations.json, req/contract_refs.json, req/structural_contracts.json, req/behavioral_contracts.json, and req/evidence_plan.json. Repair only those req JSON files if needed, preferring incremental edits (replace_in_file/replace_lines) that touch only the changed fields over a full write_file rewrite; only overwrite a whole file when its structure must be completely redone. Check that requirements are implementation/testable scope, obligations are atomic and observable, contract_refs are machine-checkable, structural_contracts define generic IO/clock/reset/interface signals plus sync/async timing domains, behavioral_contracts define decision tables/state or transaction rules plus stage checks, and evidence_plan entries name real artifact classes, deterministic validators, and pass_conditions. Do not mark files locked. Do not create locked_truth.md or approval_manifest.json.
tasks.criteria:
All six candidate JSON files exist (authored here when no draft preceded)
Each required requirement has at least one obligation_ref
Each obligation is atomic enough for pass/fail/blocked judgment
Each obligation has at least one contract_ref
Each contract_ref can map to SSOT/FL/CL/RTL/TB/SIM or an explicit future stage reflection
Each structural_contract has obligation refs, signals with name/dir/width, and timing domains for sync/async IO where applicable
Each behavioral_contract has obligation refs, machine-readable decision_table/state_transitions/transactions/rules/truth_table, and stage_contracts
Each evidence_plan entry has artifact, validator, and pass_condition
Over-broad requirements are split or listed as blockers
No locked_truth.md or approval_manifest.json was created
No implementation artifacts were modified

tasks.detail: Run the deterministic contract authority checker in review-candidate mode. This checks JSON shape, req -> obligation -> structural/behavioral contract closure, function/cycle contract semantics, and evidence_plan closure. A pass means the bundle is ready for human review, not locked.
tasks.criteria:
check_contract_bundle.py --review-candidate exits 0
req -> obligation refs close
obligation -> contract_ref refs close
Each obligation has at least one structural_contract_ref or behavioral_contract_ref; contract_refs alone are not enough
structural_contracts validate generic IO/clock/reset/interface structure and known obligation refs
behavioral_contracts validate function semantics plus cycle/timing semantics or explicit cycle waiver
contract_ref/structural_contract/behavioral_contract -> evidence_plan refs close
Evidence entries include artifact, validator, and pass_condition
No approval_manifest.json is required in this mode
No locked_truth.md is required in this mode

tasks.activeForm: Summarizing review candidate for human decision
tasks.detail: Read the validated req JSON files and report that this is ready_for_human_review only. Do not say locked or approved. Next action is human review, then /lock-req only after explicit approval.
tasks.criteria:
Review candidate status is reported as ready_for_human_review
Requirement/obligation/contract/structural_contract/behavioral_contract/evidence counts are reported
req/contract_authority_report.json or req/contract_closure.json is summarized
Human approval is listed as required
Next action is /lock-req only after explicit approval
/to-ssot and implementation are still blocked before /lock-req
